fixture_source.derivation_note: Items A and B carry source_quotes that appear verbatim in the Dec 18 transcript at tests/fixtures/llm_extraction/dec18_transcript.txt. Item C is synthetic (the real schema-1.4.0 artifact carries source_quote on every item; a null-grounding case is constructed to pin the cascade's missing-grounding behaviour). The fixture is intentionally static so CI does NOT read the data-lake.

fixture_description: Phase 2.C smoke fixture. Three meeting_minutes-shaped items exercise the cascade's keep/drop dispatch on real-shaped inputs: one well-grounded procedural_ruling (Item A), one vague action_items entry (Item B), one synthetic action_items entry with source_quote: null (Item C). The cascade groups all three into chunk 0 and asks the filter (production code + deterministic stub in CI) to keep/drop each.
chunk_text:
7 GHz Downlink TIG Meeting Kickoff - transcript 20251218

Chair Smith: Good morning everyone, this is the kickoff of the 7 GHz downlink technical interference group. Let's begin with the agenda.
NTIA Lead: Thank you. NTIA has reviewed the prior comment cycle and is ready to proceed.
Chair Smith: First item. We need to decide on the downlink power threshold for the 7 GHz band.
NTIA Lead: NTIA approved the 7 GHz downlink threshold of minus 47 dBm per megahertz.
Chair Smith: Noted. The group approved the 7 GHz downlink threshold of minus 47 dBm per megahertz.
DoD Rep: DoD has a concern about the aggregate interference methodology and would like it revisited.
Chair Smith: The group deferred the aggregate interference methodology pending further study.
NTIA Lead: We should assign someone to draft the revised methodology.
Chair Smith: Action item. DoD will submit revised ERP values before the next session.
DoD Rep: Understood. DoD will submit revised ERP values before the next session.
NTIA Lead: One open question remains. What is the coordination distance for federal incumbents in the 7 GHz band?
Chair Smith: Let's record that as an open question. What is the coordination distance for federal incumbents in the 7 GHz band?
Chair Smith: Any other business? Hearing none, we adjourn. Thank you all.

item_A.rationale: Well-grounded: long verbatim source_quote that anchors a specific threshold ruling (minus 47 dBm per megahertz) on a specific band (7 GHz). The quote appears verbatim in the transcript on Chair Smith's line. A realistic Sonnet filter would keep this item.

item.ruling_text: The group approved the 7 GHz downlink threshold of minus 47 dBm per megahertz.
item.source_quote: The group approved the 7 GHz downlink threshold of minus 47 dBm per megahertz.

item_B.rationale: Vague action: source_quote is 'Thank you.' (NTIA Lead's line), which is a routine acknowledgement and does not anchor a real action commitment. The item plausibly represents a Haiku over-extraction. A realistic Sonnet filter would drop this item.

item.action: Acknowledge NTIA's review of the prior comment cycle
item.reason: Haiku flagged this as an action; Sonnet should drop because the quote does not anchor a real commitment.

item_C.rationale: Null source_quote: pins the cascade's missing-grounding behaviour. The cascade's _locate_chunk_for_item bucket the item into chunk 0; _build_chunk_payload_for_filter sees grounding_mode='verbatim' but source_quote is None, so no _chunk_context is added; the item is sent to the filter as-is. The cascade emits NO special reason code (no 'failed:missing_source_quote'). Whatever the filter decides is applied without exception.

item.action: Follow up with stakeholders on outstanding items
item.reason: Synthetic; the real artifact carries source_quote on every item under schema 1.4.0. This case pins the cascade's null-source_quote behaviour.
